figures.caption: Figure 3: Enabledness-Preserving Abstraction (epa) graph for the Document Sharing workflow. The yellow self-loop on SHARING_WITH_THIRD_PARTY represents the bola vulnerability discovered by the semantic fuzzer at iteration 52. The transition is absent in the fixed epa after the Rego policy amendment.
Figure 5: EPA graph for the Document Sharing workflow (pre-fix). The self-loop on SHARING_WITH_THIRD_PARTY represents the unauthorized accept_sharing_request() transition discovered by the fuzzer.
Table 2: Test suite breakdown by component.
Table 1: Mapping between architectural components (paper) and implementation files (poc).

Figure 4: Semantic fuzzer execution flow. The guided mutation strategy (80% valid-tool selection, 20% adversarial) efficiently navigates toward boundary states. A violation immediately halts the campaign and reports the falsified invariant with its full call sequence.

Figure 1: Semantic Gateway architecture. The intent flows from enterprise sources through the Semantic Firewall, Embedding Router, Chain-of-Thought Planner, and Policy Enforcement Point before reaching the Tool Runtime and Audit Ledger.
Table 3: Comparative evaluation results: Semantic Gateway vs. traditional REST baseline.

Figure 2: Three-layer Zero-Trust security model. Each incoming intent must survive Layer 1 (Semantic Firewall), Layer 2 (OPA-based Tool RBAC with BOLA ownership verification), and Layer 3 (Cryptographic Human-in-the-Loop) before any tool execution is permitted.
